channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Artemis II returns LIVE: Splashdown less than six hours away as crew prepares for fireball return - Live Science
item.description:
1. Artemis II returns LIVE: Splashdown less than six hours away as crew prepares for fireball return  Live Science
  2. Live updates: Artemis II astronauts to make splashdown return to Earth  CNN
  3. Artemis II Flight Day 10: Crew Sets for Final Burn, Splashdown  NASA (.gov)
  4. Artemis 2 LIVE: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts prepare for splashdown today  Space
  5. How to watch NASA’s moon mission splash down off San Diego today  Los Angeles Times

item.title: Yellowstone's magma source may be closer than thought, reshaping hazard models - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Yellowstone's magma source may be closer than thought, reshaping hazard models  Phys.org
  2. Yellowstone’s Dreaded Supervolcano Has an Unexpected Power Source, Study Says  Gizmodo
  3. New study reveals hidden forces behind Yellowstone supervolcano  Xinhua
  4. The path of least resistance: Investigating hot spring plumbing systems in Yellowstone using electrical measurements  USGS (.gov)
  5. Scientists use new methods to map Yellowstone's hidden underground plumbing system  FOX Weather

item.title: A tiny experiment using Artemis II astronaut cells could reshape medicine - The Washington Post
item.description:
1. A tiny experiment using Artemis II astronaut cells could reshape medicine  The Washington Post

  4. What returning to lunar space means for human health  SpaceNews
  5. What 10 days of space travel could mean for Artemis astronauts' health  Sky News

item.title: Oldest octopus fossil found to not be an octopus - Ars Technica
item.description:
1. Oldest octopus fossil found to not be an octopus  Ars Technica
  2. The ‘oldest octopus’ in the world isn’t an octopus after all, scientists find  CNN
  3. "Weird blob" creature thought to be the world's oldest octopus isn't an octopus after all. Here's what scientists found.  CBS News
  4. Shocking Twist in 300-Million-Year ‘World’s Oldest’ Octopus Fossil, Scientists Reveal  SURFER Magazine
  5. 'Oldest octopus' fossil is no octopus at all, scans reveal  Phys.org

item.title: The Artemis II Effect: When Everyone Is Brilliant, Character Becomes The Mission - Forbes
item.description:
1. The Artemis II Effect: When Everyone Is Brilliant, Character Becomes The Mission  Forbes

  4. The Miracle and Mystery of Artemis II  Washington Monthly
  5. Artemis II's moon mission is the 1st in more than 50 years. Why did it take the US so long to return?  ABC News

item.title: 'The comet went in, but only a cloud of debris came out': Watch bright Comet MAPS get torn apart by the sun - Live Science
item.description:
1. 'The comet went in, but only a cloud of debris came out': Watch bright Comet MAPS get torn apart by the sun  Live Science
  2. Rare Comet Visible For First Time In 170,000 Years This Weekend  Forbes
  3. Why I'm hunting for Comet Pan-STARRS right now — before it's too late  Space
  4. Watch a Comet Get Torn Apart by the Sun and Become a Headless Ghost  Gizmodo
  5. How to Watch a Rare Comet for the First Time in 170,000 Years This Weekend  People.com

item.description: 'Little red dots' spotted by the James Webb telescope may just be awkward black holes going through a phase  Live ScienceView Full Coverage on Google News

item.title: Want a better night's sleep? Go camping - BBC
item.description: Want a better night's sleep? Go camping  BBC

item.title: Female mice grow testes after this single DNA tweak - Nature
item.description:
1. Female mice grow testes after this single DNA tweak  Nature
  2. A single-nucleotide enhancer mutation overrides chromosomal sex to drive XX male development  Nature
  3. With a switch of a letter in DNA ‘dark matter,’ Israeli scientists change sex of mouse  The Times of Israel
  4. Tiny DNA tweak flips biological sex, researchers report  The Jerusalem Post
  5. Changing 'just one DNA letter' in female mice triggers growth of male genitalia  Yahoo

item.title: Ancient human populations disappeared 3,000 years ago in France: DNA study reveals Paris tomb mystery - The Times of India
item.description:
1. Ancient human populations disappeared 3,000 years ago in France: DNA study reveals Paris tomb mystery  The Times of India
  2. Neolithic Europeans Suddenly Stopped Building Megalithic Tombs 5,000 Years Ago - And Now We Know Why  IFLScience
  3. Entire Human Populations Vanished 3,000 Years Ago. Scientists Figured Out Where They Went.  Popular Mechanics
  4. Population discontinuity in the Paris Basin linked to evidence of the Neolithic decline  Nature
  5. DNA evidence reveals a Stone Age population collapse in France  Phys.org

item.title: The Heavens Are Still Declaring—and Not Just to Astronauts - The Gospel Coalition
item.description:
1. The Heavens Are Still Declaring—and Not Just to Astronauts  The Gospel Coalition

  5. “Cosmic Orphans” No More: Stephen Meyer on the Meaning of the Artemis II Mission  Science and Culture Today

item.title: A 289-Million-Year-Old Mummified Reptile From An Oklahoma Cave Shows How We Breathe The Way We Do - IFLScience
item.description:
1. A 289-Million-Year-Old Mummified Reptile From An Oklahoma Cave Shows How We Breathe The Way We Do  IFLScience
  2. The oldest breath: A 300-million-year-old mummy reveals the origins of how amniotes breathe  EurekAlert!
  3. Mummified reptiles are revealing how breathing evolved  The Economist
  4. Permian Fossil is Earliest Evidence of Rib-Powered Breathing  Sci.News
  5. Mummified reptile offers glimpse at how breathing began  The Globe and Mail

item.title: 250-Million-Year-Old Fossil Confirms That Mammal Ancestors Laid Eggs, Solving a Long-Standing Mystery - Indian Defence Review
item.description:
1. 250-Million-Year-Old Fossil Confirms That Mammal Ancestors Laid Eggs, Solving a Long-Standing Mystery  Indian Defence Review
  2. Mammal ancestors laid eggs—and this 250-million-year-old fossil proves it  Phys.org
  3. Scientists ‘thrilled’ to find egg laid by our distant ancestors  The Times
  4. Embryo fossil found in South Africa is world’s oldest proof that mammal ancestors laid eggs  The Conversation
  5. Proto-mammals laid eggs, paleontologists finally confirm  Popular Science

item.description:

item.title: 500-million-year-old clawed predator found in Utah desert forces scientists to rethink origin of spiders - BBC Wildlife Magazine
item.description:
1. 500-million-year-old clawed predator found in Utah desert forces scientists to rethink origin of spiders  BBC Wildlife Magazine
  2. A chelicera-bearing arthropod reveals the Cambrian origin of chelicerates  Nature
  3. Fossil of Pincer-Wielding Crawler Reveals Origins of Spiders, Scorpions and Others  The New York Times
  4. This tiny claw in a 500-million-year-old fossil just rewrote the origin of spiders  ScienceDaily
  5. This 500-million-year-old fossil of claw-bearing predator uncovers origins of spiders, scorpions, other arthropods  West Virginia University

item.title: Strange Microbes Found in Shark Bay Could Reveal How Complex Life Began - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Strange Microbes Found in Shark Bay Could Reveal How Complex Life Began  ScienceAlert
  2. ‘First contact’ that may have led to complex life on Earth finally witnessed by scientists  The Conversation
  3. World-first discovery after trip to Aussie coast linked to ancient event: 'Never seen it before'  Yahoo News Australia
  4. From Asgard to Earth: Tiny tubes may reveal the moment complex life began  Phys.org
  5. A composite image of the Asgard archaeon (inset) found within the microbial mats of Shark Bay, Western Australia  EurekAlert!

item.title: 'We all pretty much broke down right there': Inside the Artemis 2 astronauts' emotional moment near the moon - Space
item.description:
1. 'We all pretty much broke down right there': Inside the Artemis 2 astronauts' emotional moment near the moon  Space
  2. Astronauts Dedicate Moon Crater to Carroll Wiseman, Wife of NASA Commander  The New York Times
  3. Artemis II crew tearfully proposes to name moon crater after astronaut Reid Wiseman's late wife  NBC News
  4. Parents Find Comfort in Bright Spot of the Moon Being Named After a NICU Nurse  TODAY.com
  5. Artemis II astronauts reflect on historic flight as they wrap up moon mission  CBS News
